title: Proxmox VE pveproxy and API Authentication Failure Spike Detection
exclusions: Proxmox Mail Gateway (PMG) and Proxmox Backup Server (PBS) which use separate authentication daemons; standalone hypervisor nodes not joined to a cluster (no /etc/pve corosync filesystem); legacy PVE 6.x (Debian 10 Buster, end-of-life mid-2024).
dataSources: `index=virtualization` with **`sourcetype="proxmox:pveproxy"`** tailing **`/var/log/pveproxy/access.log`** (Apache combined format on HTTPS port **8006**) plus optional **`proxmox:journal`** (**journalctl -u pveproxy.service**) and **`linux_secure`** **`/var/log/auth.log`** lines referencing **`pveproxy`** PAM failures; supplemental **HEC** JSON streams (**proxmox:cluster_log**) sourced from scripted polls of **`/api2/json/cluster/log`** when API-derived narratives complement proxy failures.
app: **Splunk Universal Forwarder** with **`monitor://`** stanzas per node paired with **Splunk Add-on for Linux** (**Splunk_TA_nix**, [Splunkbase 833](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)); optional **Cribl Stream**, **Vector**, **Fluent Bit**, or **HEC** collectors when syslog pipelines prefer aggregation hops before Splunk Cloud ingress.
spl:
index=virtualization sourcetype="proxmox:pveproxy" earliest=-4h
| rex field=_raw "^(?<client_ip>\S+)\s+\-\s+(?<http_user>\S+)\s+\[(?<apache_ts>[^\]]+)\]\s+\"(?<http_method>\S+)\s+(?<uri_path>\S+)\s+[^\"]+\"\s+(?<status>\d+)\s+(?<bytes>\d+)"
| eval http_status=tonumber(status)
| eval realm_suffix=case(
    http_user=="-", "missing_identity_token",
    match(http_user,".+@.+"), mvindex(split(http_user,"@"),1),
    true(), "legacy_literal_user"
  )
| eval endpoint_family=case(
    match(uri_path,"^/api2/json/access/ticket(?:\\?|$)"), "ticket_cookie_totp_exchange",
    match(uri_path,"^/api2/json/access/users"), "directory_user_handles",
    match(uri_path,"^/api2/json/cluster/(?!log)"), "cluster_config_surface",
    match(uri_path,"^/api2/json/cluster/log"), "cluster_journal_feed",
    match(uri_path,"^/api2/json/nodes/[^/]+/(qemu|lxc|tasks|storage|network|firewall|capabilities)"), "hypervisor_plane_calls",
    match(uri_path,"^/api2/json/storage(/|\\?|$)"), "disk_library_maps",
    match(uri_path,"^/api2/json/access/domains"), "realm_catalog_pull",
    true(), "misc_gui_static_or_ws"
  )
| eval failure_reason=case(
    http_status==401, "ticket_cookie_secret_otp_wrong_or_absent",
    http_status==403, "rbac_path_blocked_or_bad_csrf_header",
    http_status==596, "cannot_reach_pvedaemon_unix_socket",
    http_status>=500, "perl_stack_exception_after_tls",
    http_status==429, "rate_limit_or_login_throttle",
    true(), "status_watch_other"
  )
| eval automation_lane=case(
    match(http_user,"(?i)^(ansible|terraform|packer|jenkins|gitlab|harbor|vault)@"), "infra_pipeline_principal",
    http_user=="-", "pre_ticket_probe_or_cookie_rotation",
    match(http_user,".+\\!.+"), "misformatted_username_token_syntax_in_username_field",
    true(), "human_or_named_svcacct"
  )
| where http_status IN (401,403,596) OR (http_status IN (429,400) AND match(uri_path,"/api2/json/access/ticket"))
| eval client_ip_nat=replace(client_ip,"^::ffff:","")
| bucket _time span=5m as burst_bin
| stats count as failure_lines,
        dc(uri_path) as uri_fanout,
        values(endpoint_family) as touched_surfaces,
        values(failure_reason) as stacked_reasons,
        latest(automation_lane) as lane,
        latest(realm_suffix) as realm_observed,
        max(http_status) as worst_http,
        earliest(_time) as window_start,
        latest(_time) as window_end
    by client_ip_nat, http_user, burst_bin
| sort client_ip_nat http_user burst_bin
| streamstats window=12 global=f median(failure_lines) as rolling_med_by_src by client_ip_nat
| eval spike_ratio=failure_lines / max(rolling_med_by_src, 1)
| eval spray_pattern=case(
    uri_fanout>=12 AND failure_lines>=12, "wide_endpoint_discovery_with_bad_secret",
    uri_fanout<=2 AND failure_lines>=35, "narrow_repeat_same_ticket_route",
    spike_ratio>=6 AND failure_lines>=10, "delta_burst_vs_recent_same_src",
    failure_lines>=45, "absolute_high_watermark",
    true(), "below_programmatic_cut"
  )
| where spray_pattern!="below_programmatic_cut"
| eval suppression_lane=case(
    match(lane,"infra_pipeline_principal"), "still_review_but_expect_hot_ci",
    spray_pattern=="delta_burst_vs_recent_same_src", "possible_sleep_then_attack_cycle",
    true(), "soc_queue_candidate"
  )
| sort 0 - failure_lines
| head 150
description: Raises anomalies when **`pveproxy`** HTTPS/API plane telemetry shows coordinated spikes of **401**, **403**, **596**, or throttled **`/api2/json/access/ticket`** failures spanning brute-force guessing, LDAP/**OIDC** realm outages mistaken for crime, RBAC denials across **`/api2/json/cluster/`** subtree enumeration, misconfigured **`PVEAPIToken`** automation, or repeated **TOTP** misses tied to **`pam`** versus **`ldap`** realms.
value: Prioritizes SOC investigation before attackers escalate from noisy failures to issued tickets controlling **qemu/lxc** lifecycles, **`cluster/tasks`** mutations, or **`/storage`** exports—protecting KVM guests and Linux containers tied into regulated workloads.
implementation: Deploy UF monitors on each node, normalize combined-log extractions, maintain **`proxmox_ops_allowlist`** lookups for CI egress, schedule companion searches on successful **`access/ticket`** **200** responses, and rehearse **`journalctl`** validation whenever dashboards disagree.
visualization: (1) Heatmap: **`failure_lines`** × **`spray_pattern`** with colour intensity by **`worst_http`**. (2) Choropleth or geomap on **`client_ip_nat`** when GeoIP enrichment exists. (3) Timechart: stacked **`failure_reason`** counts across **`realm_suffix`** facets. (4) Table: **`burst_bin`**, **`client_ip_nat`**, **`http_user`**, **`uri_fanout`**, **`suppression_lane`**, **`lane`** for analyst drilldowns. (5) Overlay companion tile counting **`200`** **`ticket`** successes within ±15 minutes for credential-stuffing transitions.
knownFalsePositives: Quarterly API-token rotations coordinated via **pveum user token remove** cycles temporarily elevate **401** volumes because orchestration retries credentials across dozens of Ansible forks until fresh **PVEAPIToken** secrets propagate—pause detections during documented ceremonies or widen suppression lookups. **Terraform Cloud** workspaces firing simultaneous applies against nested modules occasionally replay stale pam-local passwords pulled from outdated workspace variables; bursts originate from SaaS egress pools that mimic attackers until workspace owners patch secrets. **Browser password wallets** autofilling retired cluster passwords during KVM console reopen drills produce clustered misses from one workstation subnet—harmless but statistically identical to spraying until HR timestamps prove benign reuse. **Brownout-induced TCP retries** on crowded management VLANs duplicate HTTPS POST attempts without malicious actors doubling guesses—cross-check switch interface counters before paging SOC leadership. **LDAP replica convergence lag** during stretched-cluster failovers rejects binds against read-only replicas while writes succeed elsewhere—realm diagnostics spike though operator intentions remain genuine. **Legacy Jenkins agents** pinned to outdated credential bindings hammer **/api2/json/access/ticket** nightly backup windows until admins rebuild freestyle jobs—annotate Jenkins hosts inside CMDB-derived lookups before interpreting spikes.
hardware: Commodity x86_64 servers — Dell PowerEdge R650/R660/R750/R760, HPE ProLiant DL360/DL380, Supermicro 2029U/6029U, Lenovo SR630/SR650, Cisco UCS C220/C240 M5/M6 running Proxmox VE 8.x
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Debian 12 hosts running Proxmox VE 8.x (7.x remains common) where **pveproxy.service** terminates TLS on **TCP/8006** and records Apache combined-format lines under **/var/log/pveproxy/access.log**. Cluster members must ship logs individually because Corosync replicates **/etc/pve** metadata—not plaintext HTTP transcripts—and attackers hop nodes during sprays.
• Splunk Universal Forwarder 9.x on every hypervisor node that participates in VIP failover, including drained hosts that still answer maintenance HTTPS probes during rolling upgrades.
• Splunk Add-on for Linux (**Splunk_TA_nix**, Splunkbase 833) to reuse mature **props** for **linux_secure** when you ship **/var/log/auth.log** lines mentioning **pveproxy** alongside HTTPS failures.
• Outbound **TCP/9997** (or your Splunk Cloud gateway) permitted from the management VLAN; SSL inspection appliances must exempt Splunk forwarding streams so UF ACK channels stay stable during noisy bursts.
• Internal CMDB mapping **pveum** principals such as **root@pam**, **PVEAuditor**, and pam-realm automation robots so SOC analysts interpret **realm_suffix** results without paging Linux admins nightly.
• Architecture diagrams tying **pvestatd**, **pvedaemon**, **pve-cluster**, and **pveproxy** interactions help tabletop participants understand why HTTP **596** storms deserve parallel infrastructure bridges—not credential hunts alone.

Step 1 — Configure log shipping and parsing
(1) Validate **systemctl status pveproxy** is active and **/var/log/pveproxy/access.log** grows when you POST credentials against **/api2/json/access/ticket** from a lab subnet. Missing logs usually indicate syslog redirection or packaging that relocated logs beneath alternate filenames—restore vendor defaults before Splunk onboarding.
(2) Place **inputs.conf** **monitor://** stanzas inside **local/** on your forwarding tier so **/var/log/pveproxy/access.log** lands in **index=virtualization** with **sourcetype=proxmox:pveproxy**. Mirror **RFC5424** syslog collectors only if you dedupe downstream Cribl pipelines—double ingestion doubles spike ratios.
(3) Optionally stream **journald** slices filtered on **UNIT=pveproxy.service** into **proxmox:journal** using TA-supported modular inputs when flat files stall during rapid **ZFS snapshot** rollback drills.
(4) Configure **props.conf** LINE_BREAKERS consistent with newline-terminated Apache logs; then promote extracted fields (**method**, **uri**, **status**) via transforms so downstream macros remain portable across clusters.
(5) Capture **PAM** denials via **monitor:///var/log/auth.log** assigned to **linux_secure**, preserving **rhost** fields that tie UNIX-layer failures back to HTTPS-layer IPs during investigations.

Step 2 — Create the detection search and schedule
Paste the SPL shown in this UC's **spl** field into a saved search scheduled every five minutes with a four-hour earliest window (stretch to twenty-four hours during green-field baselines). Scope permissions narrowly—SOC analysts need search rights on **virtualization**, not indexer administrator roles.

Search rationale:
– **client_ip_nat** collapses IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses so perimeter firewall logs agree with Splunk aggregates.
– **endpoint_family** distinguishes brute-force probes touching **cluster/** URIs versus operators stuck retrying **access/ticket** after typoed passwords.
– **spray_pattern** labels narrow repetition versus broad enumeration—adjust thresholds once honest CI/CD telemetry baselines finish.
– **failure_reason** reminds responders that HTTP **596** often reflects **pvedaemon** socket timeouts rather than guessed passwords.

Step 3 — Validate against live telemetry and UI traces
(1) **Verify against the Proxmox VE web UI** by comparing Splunk totals with interactive navigation through **Datacenter → Permissions → Users**—unexpected inactive accounts appearing in spikes merit HR coordination before blocking subnets blindly.
(2) Run **journalctl -u pveproxy.service --since "-45 min"** on the active node and confirm authentication strings align minute-by-minute with Splunk aggregates for the suspect IP.
(3) Exercise **curl -k -d 'username=user@pam&password=dummy' https://127.0.0.1:8006/api2/json/access/ticket** from a trusted shell to emit deterministic **401** rows your pipeline must ingest—failure here proves parsing gaps before blaming attackers.
(4) Pull **GET /api2/json/cluster/log** via authenticated tooling during outages to differentiate uniform OIDC outages from isolated brute-force storms (every principal fails versus one subnet hammering ticket POSTs).
(5) Compare successful **200** POST counts within ±15 minutes using a companion saved search—credential stuffing investigations rarely stop at failures alone.

Step 4 — Operationalise dashboards, lookups, and telemetry breadth
(1) Maintain **lookup tables** describing automation egress (/24 or host literals) feeding GitLab, Ansible Tower, or Terraform Enterprise runners—annotate Splunk rows with **owner** metadata via **lookup** enrichment scheduled nightly.
(2) Mirror summarized hourly CSV extracts into ticketing attachments auditors already consume so virtualization governance packs stay synchronized without bespoke portals.
(3) Route normalized metrics (**index=_metrics** via OTLP if enabled) alongside logs when Splunk Observability Cloud spans exist—latency spikes on **TCP/8006** complement brute-force hypotheses without merging unrelated KPIs into this SPL string.
(4) Publish Tableau or Splunk Dashboard Studio tiles pairing **failure_lines** with **successful_ticket_posts** during quarterly tabletop exercises referencing OWASP authentication guidance—leadership expects visuals before approving subnet bans.
(5) Coordinate specifically with backup administrators so **Proxmox Backup Server** incidents remain excluded—those stacks authenticate differently and belong to sibling scopes noted under exclusions.


Extended instrumentation guidance (between Steps 4 and 5)
Optional collectors may emit **HEC** batches sourced from **`/api2/json/cluster/tasks`** (**proxmox:task_log**) or **`/api2/json/cluster/log`** (**proxmox:cluster_log**) JSON lines. Those feeds rarely substitute **access.log** telemetry yet clarify coordinated chaos—pair **`tasks`** rows showing **`failed`** **`qmigrate`** or **`vzdump`** attempts concurrent with **`401`** bursts before tagging incidents as insider sabotage versus clumsy CI tooling. Mirror perimeter **`iptables`**/**nftables** denies forwarded with **`subsystem=pve`** hints when brute-force subnets pivot toward adjacent **SSH** bastions sharing management VLANs. Document Splunk Knowledge Objects referencing **`Splunk_TA_nix`** rename history (**Splunk_TA_unix**) so cloned stacks inherit **`props`** without silently reverting **`LINE_BREAKER`** experiments performed during proof-of-concept clusters.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting

• **Zero proxmox:pveproxy events** often means collectors monitor PBS appliances instead of PVE hosts—confirm inventory tags before altering SPL thresholds.

• Duplicate ingestion via syslog relay plus UF doubles counts—pick one authoritative transport per node.

• Load balancer VIP masks **client_ip** without **X-Forwarded-For** inserts—terminate TLS either on **pveproxy** directly or instruct HAProxy to rewrite forwarded headers Splunk can parse.

• Persistent HTTP **596** codes indicate **pvedaemon** outages or exhausted UNIX sockets—recover backend daemons prior to escalating SOC hunts.

• RBAC-only **403** bursts during firewall audits trace operators lacking **PVESDNAdmin** privileges probing **/cluster/firewall** endpoints—cross-reference change calendars.

• **realm_suffix** mismatches during Active Directory migrations occur when admins keep pam-style logins while accounts already migrated to Active Directory realms—education beats containment.

• Daylight-saving jumps stretching **burst_bin** splits confuse **rolling_med_by_src**—apply calendar-aware suppression windows twice yearly.

• Sparse labs under-power streamstats—fallback to absolute cutoffs recorded in tuning notes.

• **journal-only** ingestion hides HTTP status nuances—restore flat-file tails unless corruption mandates journal-first pipelines.

• Splunk Cloud Smartstore latency delaying searches—expand earliest boundaries temporarily during investigations.

• Uniform OIDC outages flooding **401** responses tied to expired JWKS metadata—coordinate identity teams rather than SOC bans.

Additionally, segregate rehearsals where administrators intentionally enroll **WebAuthn** credentials inside training VLANs—FIDO2 enrollment failures surface as ordinary **401** responses visually identical to guessing waves unless Splunk timestamps align with documented kiosk drills maintained alongside HR calendars. Capacity-draining exercises that exhaust RAM on **pveproxy** hosts occasionally lengthen TLS handshakes enough that impatient engineers mash browser reload buttons—suppress those subnets using weekly rehearsal lists.

• **Missing fields after TA upgrades** — Splunk Add-on refreshes occasionally rename **`LINE_BREAKER`** defaults until **`rex`** anchors slip relative to **`bytes`** positions inside **`access.log`** samples.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the Proxmox web sign-in door for bursts of wrong passwords, mistyped second-factor codes, or cranky automation secrets before strangers can shut guests down or reroute disks.

controlTest.positiveScenario: An attacker rotates through POST attempts against /api2/json/access/ticket from 203.0.113.44 while probing /api2/json/cluster/tasks, /api2/json/storage, and /api2/json/nodes/node1/qemu, generating hundreds of mixed 401 and 403 rows inside ten minutes—Splunk aggregates spike failure_lines with spray_pattern labeled wide_endpoint_discovery_with_bad_secret.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Planned root-at-pam password rotations executed sequentially by operators typing slowly produce scattered failures hours apart without uri_fanout breadth, failing numeric thresholds once below_programmatic_cut filters engage.
